subject: Sociology
title: Sacred and Profane
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Sacred and Profane.

hook.text: A wedding ring is just metal. But try tossing one in the trash and watch the reaction.

buildup.text: Durkheim split the world into sacred and profane. Sacred things are set apart and treated with awe.

discovery.text: Nothing is inherently sacred. Society decides which objects, places, and times deserve special respect.

twist.text: The profane isn't evil — it's just ordinary. Your lunch is profane. A communion wafer is sacred.

climax.text: The sacred-profane boundary is socially maintained. Cross it carelessly and you invite outrage.

quiz.question: What makes something 'sacred' according to Durkheim?
options: Society setting it apart with special meaning
